data.title: Cascade Space secures $5.9M seed funding for space communication platform, backed by various investors.
data.description: A technology company based in San Francisco called Cascade Space has revealed that it recently secured $5.9 million in seed funding. The investment round saw contributions from a variety of firms, including Nova Threshold, Undeterred Capital, Y Combinator, Night Capital, Olive Capital, Valkyrie Ventures, Karman Ventures, Palm Drive Capital, Garage Capital, TRAC, Aurelia Foundry, Hawktail, MGV.VC, Pioneer Fund, Liquid 2 Ventures, among others. This funding will be utilized to advance the company's end-to-end communication system design, test, and operation platform.

data.title: Elation survey: 39.3% of primary care physicians use AI for daily clinical documentation.
data.description: In San Francisco, Elation Health, a leading clinical technology company in primary care, disclosed findings from a recent survey of 291 primary care physicians regarding the incorporation of Artificial Intelligence (AI). The survey highlighted adoption rates, concerns, insights, and user requests surrounding AI integration in primary care. Results showed that 39.3% of physicians are currently utilizing AI in their workflows.

data.title: Lagunitas seeks "Party Legend" to combat party decline with beer, statue, $5K reward. Event attendance down 35% since 2004.
data.description: Amidst a decline in party culture, Lagunitas is set to celebrate those who continue to embrace it by introducing a new beer named after them. The company also plans to erect a statue in their honor and award $5,000 to help sustain the festivities. Based in PETALUMA, Calif., this move comes in light of a 35% decrease in social gatherings since 2004.

data.title: Tampa Bay's EasyPA wins Founders Live pitch event with JOTO PR, highlighting healthcare innovation, $1M perks for startups.
data.description: The recent Founders Live pitch event in Tampa Bay garnered national attention, providing early-stage startups with valuable exposure, investor connections, and more than $1 million in startup benefits. Organized by JOTO PR Disruptors™, the event showcased the city as a significant player in the startup ecosystem, signaling its growth and potential on a larger scale.

data.title: Investors anticipate Virtus Inv's earnings report on July 25, estimating EPS at $6.17. Shares at $213.98 with 9.59% loss in the last year. Analysts predict an Underperform rating for Virtus Inv.
data.description: Virtus Investment Partners (NASDAQ: VRTS) is set to announce its quarterly earnings on July 25, 2025. Analysts predict an earnings per share (EPS) of $6.17. Investors are hopeful for results that exceed estimates and positive guidance for the next quarter, knowing that future projections heavily impact stock prices. In the last quarter, VRTS beat EPS by $0.32 with a subsequent 0.0% drop in share price the next day. In the past four quarters, VRTS has seen mixed results in EPS versus estimates, with corresponding fluctuations in stock prices. As of July 23, VRTS shares were trading at $213.98, down 9.59% in the last 52 weeks, leading to long-term shareholder dissatisfaction. Analysts currently rate VRTS as Underperform based on 5 ratings. Investors must consider market sentiments and industry expectations.

data.title: Carter's earnings report due on 2025-07-25; EPS estimate $0.40; past performances, shares at $33.93, 52-week down 41.04%.
data.description: Carter's is set to release its quarterly earnings report on July 25, 2025, with analysts predicting an EPS of $0.40. The company previously beat EPS estimates by $0.14 in the last quarter but saw a 0.0% drop in share price the next day. Over the past year, the stock price has declined by 41.04%. Analysts have given Carter's a consensus rating of Underperform and an average one-year price target of $27.5. Investors are keen to see if the company not only surpasses the earnings estimate but also provides positive guidance for future growth. The full article is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Former AM Best President & CEO Matthew Mosher joins Pharmacists Mutual Insurance Company's board of directors in July 2025.
data.description: In Algona, Iowa on July 24, 2025, Pharmacists Mutual Insurance Company revealed the selection of Matthew Mosher to its Board of Directors. Mosher, the previous President and CEO of AM Best Rating Services, Inc., joins with extensive leadership background and substantial industry knowledge.

data.title: Wabash National is set to announce quarterly earnings, with analysts estimating EPS of $-0.33. Past performance saw EPS miss by $0.30, resulting in a 9.12% share price increase. Stock trading at $10.86 with a 52-week decrease of 49.05%. Investors await surpassing estimates and favorable guidance for the next quarter. With 1 analyst rating, consensus on Wabash National is Neutral.
data.description: Wabash National (NYSE: WNC) is set to announce its quarterly earnings on July 25, 2025. Analysts are estimating an earnings per share (EPS) of $-0.33. In the previous quarter, the company missed EPS by $0.30, resulting in a 9.12% increase in share price. Wabash National's stock was trading at $10.86 on July 23, with shares down by 49.05% over the past 52 weeks. The company has a consensus rating of Neutral from 1 analyst. Investors are eagerly awaiting the earnings report for potential stock price movements. Understanding market sentiments and guidance is crucial for investors.

data.title: Nexity reported a return to positive current operating profit of €6 million in H1 2025, with 34% growth in homebuyers. Margins restored for Urban Planning and Residential Real Estate Development. Financial reinforcement until 2028 confirmed, with net debt at €398 million at June 2025. Cost-saving plan of €100 million to be achieved by 2026, with already 92% achieved for 2025. Revenue for Services up 12%, Serviced Properties up 13%. Bond repayments of €321 million made in H1 2025. Group liquidity solid at €528 million. Financing secured until 2028 with new leverage ratios. Total character count: 693
data.description: The first-half results for 2025 for the company show a positive return to current operating profit, with €6 million achieved in H1, compared to a loss of €54 million in H1 2024. The progress was driven by improved margins in Urban Planning and Residential Real Estate Development and Services, especially Serviced Properties which saw a 12.5% margin. The company achieved 92% of its cost-savings plan target of €100 million by 2026 already in 2025. Homebuyer momentum increased by 34% in H1, with supply aligned with market trends. The company also reinforced its financial structure until 2028, repaying €321 million in bond maturities in H1. The revenue for the company decreased by 12%, totaling €1,302 million in the first half of 2025. Planning and Development revenue saw a 16% decline, while Services revenue increased by 12%. The net debt before lease liabilities increased to €398 million at the end of June 2025. Bond repayments of €321 million were made in H1, and the company secured financing until 2028 with adjusted leverage ratios.

data.title: ThetaRay launches self-service Rule Builder & Simulator for compliance teams to build, test, deploy AML rules faster.
data.description: In New York, ThetaRay has introduced a no-code Rule Builder and Simulator designed for anti-money laundering purposes. This launch empowers compliance teams by allowing them complete control over constructing, testing, and implementing AML rules more efficiently and securely. This new tool aims to accelerate the process and enhance safety in compliance procedures.

data.title: Dow Jones fell with weekly jobless claims, Netflix triggered sell rule, Tesla down, Meta eyes entry. Nasdaq rose.
data.description: On Thursday, the Dow Jones index experienced a decline following an unexpected decrease in weekly jobless claims. Additionally, Netflix encountered a significant sell rule according to Investor's Business Daily. The stock market today saw the Nasdaq rising, with Netflix triggering a short-term sell rule, Tesla facing difficulties, and Meta Platforms considering a new entry. This live coverage highlights the latest developments in the stock market, with important updates for investors.

data.title: ATOM price at $4.75 is testing support, aiming to break $5.32 resistance after a 1.27% pullback. Bullish signs indicate recovery.
data.description: The cryptocurrency ATOM is currently trading at $4.75, experiencing a slight 1.27% decline. However, indicators show bullish momentum, indicating a possible recovery towards the $5.32 resistance level.

data.title: J.D. Power finds all vehicle areas improve for first time in nearly a decade, marking 30 years of APEAL study.
data.description: In Troy, Michigan, according to J.D. Power, there has been a marked improvement in all vehicle areas for the first time in nearly a decade. This significant development comes as the company celebrates 30 years of APEAL. This positive trend indicates a general enhancement in vehicle performance and customer satisfaction across the board.

data.title: UnitedHealth to assist federal probe on Medicare billing. The conglomerate reached out to the Justice Department after reports of investigation.
data.description: The health care conglomerate revealed on Thursday that it had taken the initiative to contact the Justice Department following the emergence of reports about a government probe.

data.title: Supporters of ADA hold key level amidst a dip; Potential for a rise to $1 with recent bounce.
data.description: Last year, the number of electric vehicles sold globally reached a record high of 3.2 million, marking a significant increase from the previous year. The surge in electric vehicle sales can be attributed to various factors, including government incentives, technological advancements, and a growing awareness of the need for sustainable transportation options. This trend is expected to continue in the coming years, with experts predicting that sales of electric vehicles will continue to rise steadily. In addition, the number of charging stations for electric vehicles also saw a notable increase, reaching a total of 1.2 million globally. This infrastructure expansion is seen as a crucial step in further promoting the adoption of electric vehicles and reducing reliance on traditional gas-powered cars. Despite the challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic, the electric vehicle market has shown resilience and is poised for continued growth in the future.
